channel.title: Cadence 步频 — PT Research Signal
channel.description: Daily curated physical therapy & rehab research for clinicians. AI-scored signal from 50 sources.

item.title: The effect of exercise on clinical pain: a systematic umbrella review and meta-meta-analysis
item.description: An umbrella review and meta-meta-analysis synthesized evidence from 11 databases, confirming the consistent and significant analgesic effects of exercise across various pain conditions and exercise modalities.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Distinct Patterns of Mobility Recovery After Stroke Using Routine Clinical Data
item.description: A retrospective cohort study identified distinct mobility recovery trajectories in stroke patients using Activity Measure for Post-Acute Care (AM-PAC) Basic Mobility data from 700 patients, challenging the traditional average recovery patterns.
item.author: medRxiv

item.title: Patient Engagement, Clinical Outcomes, and Satisfaction of an Advanced Orthopedic Virtual Physical Therapy Model: A Retrospective Cohort Study.
item.description: A retrospective cohort study found that an advanced orthopedic virtual physical therapy model achieved high patient engagement and satisfaction, with 70.9% of referred patients completing full care episodes (exceeding national benchmarks of 43%) and 96% achieving Minimal Clinically Important Differences.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Pelvic floor muscle training for pelvic organ prolapse: A systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials
item.description: A systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials confirmed the efficacy of pelvic floor muscle training for pelvic organ prolapse, demonstrating significant improvements in prolapse symptoms and quality of life.
item.author: Sports Medicine

item.title: APTA Report Finds Demand for Pelvic Health Physical Therapy Outpaces Access
item.description: A new APTA report, "APTA State of Pelvic Health Physical Therapy," reveals that the demand for pelvic health physical therapy in the U.S. significantly outpaces access, highlighting workforce, access, and awareness challenges.
item.author: APTA

item.title: Effectiveness of non-pharmacological interventions for lower limb motor impairment in post-stroke hemiplegia: a systematic review and Bayesian network meta-analysis | Journal of NeuroEngineering and Rehabilitation
item.description: A systematic review and Bayesian network meta-analysis evaluated the effectiveness of non-pharmacological interventions for lower limb motor impairment in post-stroke hemiplegia, identifying key effective modalities.
item.author: Sports Medicine

item.title: Pioneering programme launched to test breakthrough spinal treatment in UK rehab centres
item.description: A pioneering program has launched in UK rehab centers to test the real-world impact of Onward Medical's ARC-EX system, a UK-approved spinal cord injury treatment combining targeted electrical spinal cord stimulation with rehabilitation exercises.
item.author: Spinal Surgery News

item.title: Do blood flow restriction exercises offer additional benefits when compared to conventional exercises in musculoskeletal rehabilitation? A systematic review and meta-analysis.
item.description: A systematic review and meta-analysis of RCTs found that low-load exercise with blood flow restriction offered additional benefits over low-load exercise and comparable efficacy to high-load exercise in improving muscle strength, disability, and pain intensity in individuals with musculoskeletal conditions.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Frontline agreement in diagnosis and treatment for musculoskeletal dysfunctions. A prospective, randomized, controlled study.
item.description: A randomized controlled trial found high agreement in diagnosis, treatment, and discharge decisions for musculoskeletal dysfunctions between physiotherapists and resident physicians compared to senior physicians in an emergency department setting.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Effects of physical exercise on spasticity in people with multiple sclerosis: A systematic review.
item.description: A systematic review analyzed the effectiveness of physical exercise interventions on spasticity in people with multiple sclerosis, including randomized controlled trials with EDSS scores ≤6.0, but found effects remain unclear due to narrative synthesis.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Quantifying Physical Activity Through Step Count Estimation Across Multiple Wearable Sensor Configurations: A Validation Study in Post-Stroke and Healthy Adults.
item.description: A validation study compared the accuracy of stride-count measurement across multiple wearable sensor configurations (ActiGraph accelerometers on wrists, hips, ankles, and pressure insoles) in 18 post-stroke and 18 healthy adults against a gold-standard instrumented treadmill, finding varying accuracy based on placement and algorithm.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Development of a Core Patient-Reported Outcome (Measures) Set for Pediatric Physical Therapy.
item.description: This study developed a core set of nine patient-reported outcomes (PROs) and corresponding measures (PROMs) for pediatric physical therapy, grouped into three domains (functioning, wellbeing, context), suitable for daily practice, research, and quality assessment.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: The Impact of Mandatory Bundled Payment on Prehabilitation Delivery for Lower Extremity Joint Replacement.
item.description: A difference-in-differences analysis found that the implementation of the Comprehensive Care for Joint Replacement (CJR) mandatory bundled payment model negatively impacted the proportion of hospitals delivering prehabilitation for lower extremity joint replacement in the US.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Follow up to the 2018 IOC consensus statement on paediatric ACL injuries: towards a broader vision of care
item.description: This editorial follows up on the 2018 IOC consensus statement on pediatric ACL injuries, examining recent advances and challenges in prevention, individualized management decisions, evolving surgical strategies, and long-term holistic care.
item.author: BJSM

item.title: Multimodal Integration of Gait, Balance, and Infrared Thermography Enhances Machine-Learning Classification of Knee Osteoarthritis: A Cross-Sectional Study
item.description: A cross-sectional study found that multimodal integration of gait, balance, and infrared thermography enhanced machine-learning classification of knee osteoarthritis compared to single-domain models.
item.author: Archives of PM&R

item.title: Prehabilitation Improves Surgical Outcomes in Older Adults Undergoing Spinal Fusion: Study
item.description: A randomized clinical trial found that a multimodal prehabilitation program before spinal fusion surgery in older adults was associated with a lower risk of postoperative complications and a shorter hospital stay, but the control group demonstrated better physiological function at 90-day follow-up.
item.author: Medical Dialogues

item.title: Immediate Weight-Bearing and Early Ambulation Rehabilitation After Femoral Neck System Fixation for Undisplaced Femoral Neck Fractures: A Prospective Randomized Controlled Trial
item.description: A prospective randomized controlled trial found that immediate weight-bearing and early ambulation rehabilitation after femoral neck system fixation for undisplaced femoral neck fractures was safe and effective, promoting earlier functional recovery.
item.author: Sports Medicine

item.title: The Impact of a Single Bout of Antagonist Stretching on Agonist Muscle Performance and Range of Motion: A Systematic review and Meta-analysis
item.description: A systematic review and meta-analysis evaluated the impact of a single bout of antagonist stretching on agonist muscle performance and range of motion, aiming to resolve conflicting findings in the literature.
item.author: Sports Medicine

item.title: Validity and reliability of remote administration of the one-leg stand, timed up and go and 30-second sit-to-stand via video call in patients with lumbar spinal stenosis awaiting decompression surgery
item.description: A study validated the reliability of remote administration of the one-leg stand, timed up and go, and 30-second sit-to-stand tests via video call in patients with lumbar spinal stenosis, showing strong agreement with in-person assessments.
item.author: Sports Medicine

item.title: Identifying Gaps in Rehabilitation Strategies for Chronic Stroke: A Systematic Review of Evidence in Patients With and Without Nerve Transfer.
item.description: A systematic review compared upper limb rehabilitation strategies and outcomes in chronic stroke patients with and without contralateral C7 nerve transfer, identifying key evidence gaps in rehabilitation dose, content, and delivery models.
item.author: PubMed
